question: Look at the picture. A ____ is flying over the houses.
questionZh: 看圖片。一___正在房屋上空飛翔。

question: When I was a teenager, I was very ____ .  But now, it’s easier for me to talk to people.
questionZh: 當我是青少年時，我非常___。但現在，與人交談對我來說比較容易了。

question: Lena doesn’t want to go ____ with John because she is afraid of water.
questionZh: Lena不想和John一起去___，因為她怕水。

question: Cindy enjoys ____ her dad read stories to her before bed.
questionZh: Cindy喜歡___爸爸在睡前念故事給她聽。

question: Dad is busy cooking in the kitchen.  Dinner will be ____ in ten minutes.
questionZh: 爸爸正在廚房裡忙著做飯。晚餐十分鐘後就___了。

question: There are so many new ____ in the office.  It’ll take me some time to remember who is who.
questionZh: 辦公室裡有好多___。我需要一些時間才能記住誰是誰。

question: I feel like a ____ .  I was looking for my keys for hours but they have been in my pocket the whole time.
questionZh: 我覺得自己像個___。我找了好幾個小時的鑰匙，但它們一直都在我的口袋裡。

question: Mr. and Mrs. Wu have three daughters.  Two are in high school, and ____ is in elementary school.
questionZh: 吳先生和吳太太有三個女兒。兩個在高中，___在小學。

optionsZh.B: 另一個（三者中的另一個）

question: It is hard for trees to ____ along this beach because of the strong winds from the sea.
questionZh: 由於來自海上的強風，樹木很難在這片海灘沿岸___。

question: Christmas ____ and I want to visit my aunt abroad.  Do you have any plans yet?
questionZh: 聖誕節___，我想去國外探望阿姨。你有什麼計畫了嗎？

question: Jo won’t be happy if you’re late for his party tonight, so ____ sure that you arrive on time.
questionZh: 如果你今晚的他的派對遲到，Jo不會高興的，所以___確定你準時到達。

question: You may have a long drive because of the terrible ____ .  There are usually a lot of cars and buses during this time.
questionZh: 由於嚴重的___，你可能需要開很長的車程。在這個時段通常有很多轎車和公車。

question: In the future, there will ____ be greater basketball players than Stephen Curry, but now we believe he is the best!
questionZh: 未來___會出現比Stephen Curry更偉大的籃球員，但目前我們相信他是最好的！

question: I guess the rainwater has come in from the kitchen.  See? ____ of the windows are closed except the one in the kitchen.
questionZh: 我猜雨水是從廚房進來的。你看？___窗戶都關著，除了廚房那扇。

question: ____ machines have been used to pick fruits for a long time, they were not used on strawberry farms until several years ago.
questionZh: ___機器已被用來採摘水果很長一段時間了，但直到幾年前，它們才被用在草莓農場上。

question: There are online videos that teach you exercises you can do at home.  They’ll ____ you a trip to the gym, and some money too.
questionZh: 有一些線上影片教你在家裡做運動。它們能___你去健身房的時間，還有金錢。

question: Jane’s parents are always happy to see their grandchildren, but mine ____ less so when I visit them with my kids.
questionZh: Jane的父母總是很高興見到他們的孫子孫女，但我的父母當我帶著孩子去看望他們時___不那麼熱情。

question: Before she ____ about it, you should tell Daphne you broke her favorite cup.
questionZh: 在她___之前，你應該告訴Daphne你打破了她最喜歡的杯子。

question: It was very windy this morning.   Some of the shirts on the balcony were blown away ____ in the pond.
questionZh: 今天早上風非常大。陽台上的一些衣服被吹走，___到池塘裡了。

passage:
Rex is a dog that lives at the bakery next to our school.  He is cute and friendly.
Every morning, he stands in front of the school to welcome everybody.  We all see Rex
as one of us.  But one Monday morning, I was surprised that Rex was not there to say
hello to us.  “Rex is in the hospital.  He was hit by a car last night,” my classmate told me.
We went to see Rex in the hospital that day after school.  Two weeks later, Rex was much
better, and we decided to take him for a walk every evening.  Because of the exercise, Rex
became healthier and stronger.  Soon he could happily run and jump again.  He is now as
handsome and strong as before!
question: What happened to Rex?
questionZh: Rex發生了什麼事？

options.C: By taking him to see a doctor.
options.D: By looking for a new home for him.

question: What do we know about Mark from the dialogue?

options.B: He is looking for a new job.
options.C: He did not like Linda’s cake.
options.D: He is getting married to Jenny.

questionZh: 從對話中，我們對Mark有什麼了解？

question: Which is most likely an example of stealing someone＇ ____ s thunder? ____  likely 可能

options.A: Dennis never changes his mind except when his wife tells him to.
options.B: Melisa tells Tom she’ll go to the party but tells her mom she’ll stay home.
options.C: Jeff tells everyone he’ll move abroad when Ivy is still telling them about her baby.
options.D: Alisa says she doesn’t care what we have for lunch but also doesn’t like the restaurant

questionZh: 下列哪一個最有可能是「搶別人風頭」的例子？

optionsZh.A: Dennis從不改變主意，除非他太太叫他這麼做。
optionsZh.B: Melisa告訴Tom她會去派對，卻告訴媽媽她會留在家裡。
optionsZh.C: 當Ivy還在告訴大家她的嬰兒消息時，Jeff卻宣布他要移居海外。
optionsZh.D: Alisa說她不在乎我們午餐吃什麼，但又不喜歡那家餐廳。

question: What can you do with a White Lake City Card?

options.B: Visit any public museum in the city for free.
options.C: Take a train to places out of the three zones.
options.D: Move around the city by metro as much as you want.

questionZh: 你可以用White Lake City Card做什麼？

optionsZh.A: 兒童火車票享有八折優惠。
optionsZh.B: 免費參觀市內任何公立博物館。
optionsZh.C: 搭火車前往三個區域以外的地方。
optionsZh.D: 無限次搭乘地鐵在市區移動。

question: Stacy is going to White Lake City and is staying at a hotel near the White Lake Main Station.  She wants to visit the Museum of White Lake City History on Friday and see White Lake on Saturday.  If she plans to buy (a) White Lake City Card(s), which of the four choices will be best for her and cost her the least?

options.C: A 1-day Card for Zone 1 and a Weekend Card for Zones 1-2.
options.D: A 1-day Card for Zones 1-2 and a Weekend Card for Zones 1-2.

questionZh: Stacy要去White Lake City，住在White Lake Main Station附近的飯店。她想週五參觀White Lake City歷史博物館，週六去看White Lake。如果她打算購買City Card，哪個選擇對她最划算？

optionsZh.C: 第一區一日卡＋第一至二區週末卡。
optionsZh.D: 第一至二區一日卡＋第一至二區週末卡。

passage:
At 5 o’clock every morning, while most people are sleeping,
16-year-old Libby Larson is already up.  She mops the floor, gets
the mail and the newspapers, and gets ready for visitors to Rolling
Acres.   Libby’s grandparents started Rolling Acres in 1970, and
since then all of the Larson family have been working there.
Rolling Acres used to be just a sheep farm, but now, with its fruit
trees, vegetable gardens, and many other kinds of animals, it has
become a popular place for families to go on vacation.  Visitors can
pick fruits, collect eggs, and feed the animals.  It is one of Libby’s
jobs to show kids how to feed the baby sheep.
For her work, Libby is paid $10 an hour.  This summer, she expects to make about $1,200
and plans to buy a cell phone.  Her friends think it’s great to make that much money in a
summer.  However, for Libby, working at a family business also means making sacrifices.
“I’d love to sleep until noon or go to summer camp like other kids.  My friends stopped
inviting me on trips because I’m always busy in the summer.”  However, Libby also said,
“But it’s better to see both sides.  Working with your family is great.  You feel closer to
each other.”
question: What is Rolling Acres?
questionZh: Rolling Acres是什麼？

options.A: What Libby does at Rolling Acres.
options.B: What visitors think of Rolling Acres.
options.C: Why Libby’s grandparents started Rolling Acres.
options.D: What the Larson family’s plans are for Rolling Acres.

optionsZh.C: Libby的祖父母為什麼創辦Rolling Acres。

options.B: Getting to know different sides of your family.
options.C: Making excuses for failing to do something difficult.
options.D: Giving up something important to do something else.

options.B: Follow the old ways of life.
options.C: Save our planet before it’s too late.
options.D: Treat others the way we want to be treated.

passage:
Many years ago, Dan Ariely bought a cabinet from IKEA.  IKEA is a
famous furniture store that sells boxes of furniture parts.  People who
buy them have to put all the parts together themselves.  It took Ariely hours to build
his cabinet.  Ariely didn’t really enjoy himself when he was trying to put all the parts
together.  However, after he finished, he felt quite good about himself and his cabinet.
Later, he found that he loved his cabinet more than any of his other furniture.
Ariely wanted to know if anyone shared his feelings, so he did
a fun origami study.  He invited two groups of people to join his
study.  He called one group “builders” and the other group “buyers.”
Builders were told to make origami, like paper frogs or paper birds.  When they
finished, they were asked how much they would spend on their works.  Ariely then
showed the origami to buyers and asked them how much they would pay for them.
The two groups gave very different answers.  Though buyers would pay only five
cents for builders’ origami, builders would spend 25 cents!  Interestingly, builders
believed others would think their works were good enough to cost that much money!
Ariely’s study showed that he was not alone in his IKEA experience — we just love
things more, sometimes blindly, when we make them ourselves.  Ariely called it “the
IKEA effect.”
effect 效應 ____ furniture 家具
question: What did Ariely try to find out in the origami study?

options.A: If he could stop the IKEA effect.
options.C: Why IKEA furniture is so famous.
options.D: If anyone shared his IKEA experience.

questionZh: Ariely在摺紙研究中想找出什麼？

optionsZh.D: 是否有人有相同的IKEA經驗。

question: Which is true about the origami study?

options.A: Buyers would spend less on builders’ origami than builders would.
options.B: Builders and buyers needed to decide a price on the origami they made.
options.C: Builders knew others would not pay as much for the origami as they would.
options.D: Buyers would spend more on builders’ origami after knowing how they were made.

questionZh: 關於摺紙研究，下列哪一項是正確的？

optionsZh.A: 買方願意為製作者的摺紙花的錢比製作者少。
optionsZh.B: 製作者和買方需要共同決定摺紙的價格。
optionsZh.C: 製作者知道別人不會付和他們一樣多的錢。
optionsZh.D: 買方在了解製作過程後，願意花更多錢購買摺紙。

question: Jerry just can’t get his daughter Mia to eat more vegetables at dinner. Every time he tries to do so, there is always a lot of shouting and crying. If Jerry wants to have Mia eat more vegetables by using the IKEA effect, what should he do?

options.A: Tell Mia that he cooks the vegetables just for her.
options.B: Ask Mia to help him cook vegetables for her meal.
options.C: Give Mia her favorite candy after she eats vegetables.
options.D: Ask Mia what vegetables she likes and cook them for her.

questionZh: Jerry無法讓女兒Mia多吃蔬菜，每次嘗試都以吵鬧收場。如果Jerry想利用IKEA效應讓Mia多吃蔬菜，他應該怎麼做？

optionsZh.A: 告訴Mia他專門為她煮蔬菜。
optionsZh.B: 請Mia幫忙一起為她的餐點烹煮蔬菜。
optionsZh.C: 在Mia吃完蔬菜後給她最愛的糖果。
optionsZh.D: 詢問Mia喜歡什麼蔬菜，然後為她烹煮。

question: What is the reading mainly about?

options.A: The tips on using a picture to tell a story.
options.B: The change that electricity brought to people’s lives.
options.C: The history behind the picture of a UK electricity worker.
options.D: The story of a famous UK electricity worker from the 1970s.

questionZh: 這篇文章主要在說什麼？

optionsZh.B: 電力為人們生活帶來的改變。
optionsZh.C: 一張英國電力工人照片背後的歷史。
optionsZh.D: 一位1970年代知名英國電力工人的故事。

question: In the UK in the 1970s, what did people most likely think of the UK electricity workers?

options.A: They were not brave enough to fix their problems.
options.B: They worked like robots and never learned to change.  likely 可能
options.C: They were asking too much and did not know when to stop.
options.D: They did not care whether their job might hurt people’s health.

questionZh: 在1970年代的英國，人們對英國電力工人最可能有什麼看法？

optionsZh.A: 他們不夠勇敢，無法解決自己的問題。
optionsZh.B: 他們像機器人一樣工作，從不學習改變。
optionsZh.C: 他們要求太多，不知道何時該停止。
optionsZh.D: 他們不在乎工作是否可能傷害人們的健康。

question: Why does the writer put the “ ” mark around the word dark in the last sentence?

options.A: To say that the “dark” time was actually not dark.
options.B: To tell people that the word was said by the government.
options.C: To mean both the days without lights and the difficult lives people lived.
options.D: To show that the government and the electricity workers both lost their fights.

questionZh: 作者為什麼在最後一句的「dark」這個詞加上引號？

optionsZh.A: 表示「黑暗」時期其實並不黑暗。
optionsZh.B: 告訴讀者這個詞是政府說的。
optionsZh.C: 同時代表沒有燈光的日子和人們艱難的生活。
optionsZh.D: 說明政府和電力工人都輸了各自的鬥爭。

passage:
Most kids want gifts from their parents on their birthdays, but Cameron doesn’t.  On his
birthday, he gives his mom a gift.  He 38 doing this in his first year of junior high
school.  That year, during one class, he watched a video about how a baby is pushed from its
mother’s body.  After class, 39 .  How brave a woman is to have a baby!  Thank God I’ll
never have to do that!
When Cameron came home that day, he held his mom in his arms for a long time.  He
decided that his birthday 40 .  During those nine months inside his mom, he just ate and
slept while his mom was doing all the hard work.  So why should he get a birthday gift for
doing nothing?  If anyone should get a gift on his birthday, it should be his mom.
The 41 “birthday gift” Cameron prepared was a bag of cookies he baked.  The cookies
tasted so bad, but his mom said they were the best thing she ever got.  Hearing that 42 .
It was so much better than getting a gift.  Now, on his birthday every year, Cameron makes
a gift for his mother to thank her for giving him life.
This year, Cameron 43 his mom a nice dress.  A few months ago, he learned to make
dresses at school and decided to make one for his mom.  Now the dress is finished and
hanging behind Cameron’s bedroom door.  He believes the gift will tell his mother how
much he loves her.
questionZh: 請選出填入空格的最佳答案。

Most kids want gifts from their parents on their birthdays, but Cameron doesn’t.  On his
birthday, he gives his mom a gift.  He 38 doing this in his first year of junior high
school.  That year, during one class, he watched a video about how a baby is pushed from its
mother’s body.  After class, 39 .  How brave a woman is to have a baby!  Thank God I’ll
never have to do that!
When Cameron came home that day, he held his mom in his arms for a long time.  He
decided that his birthday 40 .  During those nine months inside his mom, he just ate and
slept while his mom was doing all the hard work.  So why should he get a birthday gift for
doing nothing?  If anyone should get a gift on his birthday, it should be his mom.
The 41 “birthday gift” Cameron prepared was a bag of cookies he baked.  The cookies
tasted so bad, but his mom said they were the best thing she ever got.  Hearing that 42 .
It was so much better than getting a gift.  Now, on his birthday every year, Cameron makes
a gift for his mother to thank her for giving him life.
This year, Cameron 43 his mom a nice dress.  A few months ago, he learned to make
dresses at school and decided to make one for his mom.  Now the dress is finished and
hanging behind Cameron’s bedroom door.  He believes the gift will tell his mother how
much he loves her.

options.A: should be spent helping his mom
options.B: should be a time of welcoming a new start
options.C: should not be about him, but about his mom
options.D: should not be celebrated for just one day, but for nine months

questionZh: 請選出填入空格的最佳答案。

optionsZh.A: 應該花在幫助他的媽媽上
optionsZh.B: 應該是歡迎新開始的時刻
optionsZh.C: 不應該是關於他，而是關於他的媽媽
optionsZh.D: 不應該只慶祝一天，而是應該慶祝九個月
